title: Breitmann As An Uhlan. III. Breitmann and Bouilli
content:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Trï؟½s estimï؟½ ami, Ick seyn nock nit verdorb,
آ آ آ آ Vielleickt Sie denck wohl kar, das ick sey tod gestorb,
آ آ آ آ Ock ne Kott loben Danck, ick leb nock kanss wohl auf.
آ آ آ آ . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
آ آ آ آ Naturlich wie Kespenst die off die Kasse keh.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ - Deutsch-Franzos, Leipzig, 1736.

آ آ آ آ Vot roombles down de Bergstrass?
آ آ آ آ Vot a grash ish in de air!
آ آ آ آ Mit a desberate gonfusion,
آ آ آ آ Und a gry of wild tespair,
آ آ آ آ Das sind gethrï؟½sht Franzosen,
آ آ آ آ Und dose who after flee
آ آ آ آ Are de terror of Champagner,
آ آ آ آ Die Uhlan cavallrie.

آ آ آ آ So liddle say die hoonted,
آ آ آ آ De hoonters lesser shdill;
آ آ آ آ Der Frank is ride forï؟½s leben,
آ آ آ آ Der Deutscher rides to kill.
آ آ آ آ Ofer dickly-doosty faces
آ آ آ آ Deir eyes like wild-katzs glare;
آ آ آ آ De blut und iron ridin
آ آ آ آ Of furie und despair.

آ آ آ آ Boot of all de wild Uhlanen,
آ آ آ آ Der Breitmann ride de pest;
آ آ آ آ For he mark de Frï؟½ntsch gommanter
آ آ آ آ Ish most elegandtly tresst.
آ آ آ آ Und ash he coom down on him,
آ آ آ آ Dereï؟½s a deatï؟½ look in his eye:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Gotts! if I carfe dat toorkey,
آ آ آ آ How Iï؟½ll make de stoofin vly!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Mit a clotter und a flotter
آ آ آ آ Like a hell-sturm dey are on:
آ آ آ آ Mit a rottle to de pattle
آ آ آ آ Coom de Deutschers, knockinï؟½ down,
آ آ آ آ Down de moundain to a bruckï؟½
آ آ آ آ Vhy die Frï؟½ntschmen toorn ad bay?
آ آ آ آ Oder Deutsch were dere pefore dem,
آ آ آ آ Und die pridge ish coot avay!

آ آ آ آ Von second der Franzose
آ آ آ آ Look down mit blitzen eye;
آ آ آ آ Von second at de bruckï؟½,
آ آ آ آ Den toorn him round to die.
آ آ آ آ Vhile mit out-ge-poke-te lanze,
آ آ آ آ Like ter teufel shot from hell,
آ آ آ آ Rode der ploonder-shtarvin Breitmann
آ آ آ آ On der grau-bart Colonel.

آ آ آ آ Vot for der Coptain Breitmann
آ آ آ آ Ish shdop in his career?
آ آ آ آ Vot for he pool his pridle?
آ آ آ آ Vot for let down his speer?
آ آ آ آ Vot for his eyes like saucers
آ آ آ آ Grow pigger, rimmed mit staub?
آ آ آ آ Vot for his hair, a pristlin,
آ آ آ آ Lift oop his pickel-haub?

آ آ آ آ So awfool so oneartï؟½ly,
آ آ آ آ So treadful was his glare,
آ آ آ آ So unbeschreiblich gastly,
آ آ آ آ Dat der Colonel self was shkare.
آ آ آ آ Oop come der Breitmann ridin,
آ آ آ آ Und mit gratin force he said:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Bist du wirkelich lebendig?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Can de grafe gife oop its tead?

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Dou livest yet dou breafï؟½st yet,
آ آ آ آ Dough oldter now you pe
آ آ آ آ Since I mordered you in Strasburg,
آ آ آ آ Mein freund mon Jean Bouilli.
آ آ آ آ We lofed de selfe maiden
آ آ آ آ Wohl forty years agone:
آ آ آ آ She died to hear I kilt you:
آ آ آ آ Jean how weiss your beard ish grown!

آ آ آ آ ï؟½I would gife my Habï؟½ und Gï؟½ter,
آ آ آ آ Dereto mein bit of life
آ آ آ آ Couldt I pring dat shild to leben,
آ آ آ آ Und make her, Jean, dy wife!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Here der Breitmann boorst out gryin,
آ آ آ آ Like a liddle prook vept he;
آ آ آ آ Und dey hugged and gissed einander,
آ آ آ آ Der Breitmann und Bouilli.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Ach, de efils dat from efil
آ آ آ آ Troo a life ish efer grow!
آ آ آ آ Had I nefer dink I killed you,
آ آ آ آ Many a man were livin now-
آ آ آ آ Many a man dat shleeps in cane-brakes,
آ آ آ آ Many a man py pillow-shore;
آ آ آ آ For dy morder mate me reckelos,
آ آ آ آ Und von tead man gries for more!

آ آ آ آ ï؟½O Mï؟½dchen! schï؟½n im Himmel!
آ آ آ آ (Warst schon on eartï؟½ difine)
آ آ آ آ Canï؟½st dink among de Engeln
آ آ آ آ Of soosh as me und mine?
آ آ آ آ Den look on soosh a Reue,
آ آ آ آ Ash eartï؟½ has nefer known:
آ آ آ آ Whereto hast dou a sabre?
آ آ آ آ Wherefore not kill me, Jean?ï؟½

آ آ آ آ ï؟½O, ne pleurez pas, mon Breitmann!
آ آ آ آ Je trouve cela trop fort,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Gry der Colonel sehr politely;
آ آ آ آ ï؟½How! you crois dat I was mort!
آ آ آ آ Mon Dieu! ï؟½Tis but one minute,
آ آ آ آ As we galloped to this plain,
آ آ آ آ I thought your spear, mon gaillard,
آ آ آ آ Would kill me oï؟½er again.

آ آ آ آ Je vous fais mon compliment,
آ آ آ آ Your tendresse becomes you well;
آ آ آ آ Et ne pleurez pas, mon brave,
آ آ آ آ Pour la petite demoiselle.
آ آ آ آ I have had a thousand since;
آ آ آ آ One can always find such game;
آ آ آ آ Et pour dire la vï؟½ritï؟½,
آ آ آ آ I have quite forgot her name.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Der Breitmann lok so earnest,
آ آ آ آ Long and earnest at his foe,
آ آ آ آ Ash if seein troo his augen
آ آ آ آ To de forty years ago.
آ آ آ آ Mit vot a shmile der Breitmann
آ آ آ آ Toorned roundt und rode away:
آ آ آ آ Dat was all his parting greetin
آ آ آ آ To der Cï؟½lonï؟½l Franï؟½ais.
meaning:
About: Charles Godfrey Leland was an American humorist and folklorist, born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. He was educated at Princeton University and in Europe.

Leland worked in journalism, travelled extensively, and became interested in folklore and folk linguistics, publishing books and articles on American and European languages and folk traditions. Leland worked in a wide variety of trades, achieved recognition as the author of the comic Hans Breitmannï؟½s Ballads, fought in two conflicts, and wrote what was to become a primary source text for Neopaganism half a century later, Aradia, or the Gospel of the Witches.


Source:- Wikipedia.
